32033.name: Angled Shaft
32033.description: The floor beneath you is covered with a fine layer of sand and gravel. This, combined with the steep angle of the shaft, makes for hazardous travel. Well, if you fall, you'll just slide down into the river to the east and drown. No big deal.

32034.name: Angled Shaft
32034.description: Crude stone steps have been chiseled into the rock here, allowing a difficult ascension to the surface. The sound of rushing water whispers to the east, an invitation to the underground world.

25098.name: The Inclined Plains
25098.description: If these plains were completely frictionless then an object would accelerate down them at a rate proportional only to their inclination and independent of the object's mass.

25099.name: The Hogback
25099.description: The plains rise suddenly and sharply where they are topped by a barren ridge of slowly crumbling granite. The road takes advantage of one narrow fissure that bisects this ridge, another merging into a narrow hole in the mountainside.

24113.name: A Cave in the Cleft
24113.description: A tiny nook inside of the mountain cleft offers a quick escape from the sun and the elements. A whistle of wind originates from the cramped opening and swirls around inside the cave.

24007.name: Western Plains
24007.description: Coarse, brown grass is the only vegetation you can see on this rather barren plain. A sharp, bony ridge is notched by the road to the south, and the plains rise in steppes to the east.

24008.name: High Plains
24008.description: A vast range of mountains stretches across the horizon from east to west. A narrow canyon cuts into them directly to the north. The wide plains continue to the east.

24011.name: High Plains
24011.description: You stand in the midst of a vast open plain, bordered on the north by a chain of mountains. The plain marches, unbroken, toward the west and you can see some small hills to the east.

24012.name: Foothills
24012.description: Travel is fairly easy in these rolling hills. They are fairly barren, with some clumps of bunch-grass being the only vegetation. To the east is a mountain pass; vast plains spread out to the west.

24013.name: Low Mountain Pass
24013.description: You stand in an east to west mountain pass. The mountains to your north are scarred giants, but the smaller mountains to your south get even smaller as you look to the west. A path winding upward ascends eastward, toward a broken archway high above. To the north a cart-marked trail leads into the mountains, and just below you a narrow shaft plunges underground.

24024.name: Treeline
24024.description: You have reached the altitude where just a few misshapen trees manage to cling to life. A steep trail climbs up into some hills to the north. The land descends to the south and you see a greater abundance of trees in that direction. A thin trail leads along a wooded ridge to the west.

its leaves fragrant and bitter.

24025.name: Among the Thin Trees
24025.description: The altitude is pretty high here so the trees around you are all conifers, and spindly ones at that. As you look into the far distance of the north, you see that the trees evidently disappear as the landscape rises. A wide, comfortable trail leads north and south, and a shadowed trail leads to the east.

24026.name: Pine Forest
24026.description: A thick carpet of pine needles carpet the trail you tread upon. There is no wind here and the air is thick with the sharp, spicy scent of pine. The trees are closely clustered, yet you appear to be able to pass north, west. A long, slender path southward leads into a mixture of pine and oak.

24027.name: Pine Forest
24027.description: You have come to the periphery of the pine forest. Looking south, you can see rolling hills, rich with grass and wild flowers. Narrow paths lead both east and west.

32039.name: Brink
32039.description: A deep excavation stretches out beneath you, its floor lost in a pall of swirling dust. A wooden ladder pokes above the lip, inviting entry, and a barren expanse of rock-strewn hills marches eastward.

24009.name: Switchback Trail
24009.description: Snow lies in patches of shadow and lees of rocky outcroppings. A brisk wind blows along this east-west cleft with gusting, hoarse regularity. You see the spoor of elk and its constant and unwanted companion, wolf.

24022.name: Steep Slope
24022.description: This slope continues south and down to some rough, low lying hills. It also climbs steeply up into mountains to the north.

24023.name: The Rocky Hills
24023.description: The rough terrain here makes travel difficult. The predominant vegetation consists of coarse, brown weeds and low, ragged bushes. A traveled path leads north and climbs toward some mountains in the distance. A steep trail leads down and toward the south, while a narrow path ventures eastward through the rocky hills, and a beaten path heads toward what appears to be a chasm to the west. A delicate golden flower opens toward the sky, its petals catching the light.

24100.name: Narrow Cleft
24100.description: Here the rocks close, blocking further passage into the cleft. Small, furry creatures have built several nests in the sheltered niches of the cleft, and scurry at your intrusion. The winds whistle to the west, pulling with them an odd, foul scent which drifts up through a large crack in the rocks nearby.

go through.to_room_name: At the Bottom of the Cleft

24010.name: The Tumbled Archway
24010.description: Wind slices through the crumbling remains of an ancient stone wall that, to this day, bears parts of the arched roof that covered and protected this part of the trail from the scathing elements. Capitaled, ribbed granite columns lie in sections to the north and south of the trail.

24014.name: High Mountain Pass
24014.description: The mountains bordering this pass are all crowned with white. Ice crystals sting your face as they are flung at you by the wind. To the east, the pass empties into a basin. To the west, the pass descends to meet another pass further down. A precipitous path leads up the rocky face.

24015.name: Tundra Basin
24015.description: You have entered a semi-circular basin, filled with rough bushes and clumps of grass. A mountain pass is to the west and a narrow trail leads out of the basin to the east. The basin continues to the north.

24016.name: Narrow Trail
24016.description: This ghost of a trail doesn't seem to be heavily traveled. The detritus of many rock slides litters the ground. The trail enters a basin to the west and continues to the east. A delicate golden flower opens toward the sky, its petals catching the light.

24017.name: Mountain Path
24017.description: This mountain path skirts the north side of a rock outcropping which appears to bear some man-made symbols. The trail continues to the south and to the west. A narrow seam of copper glints from the rock face.

24018.name: Mountain Path
24018.description: The wind blows fiercely cold here, as if it were being channeled along this mountain trail. Looking south, you can see that the path rises slightly to the tip of a promontory. The trail also continues north.

24019.name: The Overlook
24019.description: You have stepped out onto a precipice which overlooks the plains and valleys below. The view is breath-taking, but it looks like an awful long fall. Trails lead north and east.

24020.name: Mountain Trail
24020.description: You have reached an intersection of trails. A well traveled path leads down toward the south and a narrow path, through a cleft in the mountains, leads east. A wider trail leads west. A delicate golden flower opens toward the sky, its petals catching the light.

24021.name: Narrow Cleft
24021.description: Rock faces press in tightly, as if they were intent on crushing you. The cleft continues to the east, but looking west, you see that the trail widens out.
